sentences_input.text: I was sitting there by myself the whole day, and everybody-- everybody, a few people-- that knew me there and this cousin of mine there-- [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] I went the next day to work, because I was working at this hospital.
But it meets like a part of me was cut off from me.
But as they say, I'm not-- I'm not in a-- in a position right now with the rush that we have to put myself into that.

And this is what I was thinking, that essentially, if you want to have this real in-depth interview to make sense [INAUDIBLE] the whole war, which has nothing to do maybe [INAUDIBLE] with-- it has nothing to do with the kind of thing that you are interested in, because for me, part of it, like going with them to Majdanek and translating to them, or visiting the Chopin Street store, where the Germans had put eyeglasses, which you knew that they were a pair of eyeglasses belong to a human being, or toothbrushes, or whatever.
And the German exact way as they usually did things, to go through this particular-- or to-- to have the interviews with the children that I mentioned that I have made--
But I'm telling it to you like it's a summary, not putting my-- my feelings and all that.
To do it in a rush, rush, rush way, that's not-- that's all what I can say.
It's not-- it's not that I'm not telling you what's-- what's happening or what I saw, but I'm not-- I'm not putting my emotions into it, not-- not as [INAUDIBLE] as I-- as I should, maybe.
